channel.title: Linux on JF
channel.description: Recent content in Linux on JF

item.title: Install Custom Applications on Linux
item.description:
Overview

When using Linux machines where you lack root privileges (sudo), you can still install applications locally. This guide covers how to set up standalone binaries (like VS Code, Obsidian, or custom tools) and integrate them into your desktop environment’s app launcher using .desktop files.

Step-by-Step Installation

1. Download and Prepare

Download the executable or compressed archive (.tar.gz, .zip, .AppImage) for your application. If it’s a folder, move it to a dedicated local bin directory.

item.title: Use Neoylayout
item.description:
This is a short summary of the essentials of the keybord layout neo-layout variant bone for faster and healtier text (and math) typing on your PC.

Windows

To use it on Windows follow these steps.

Install

  • Copy the kbdbone.dll file in System32 to C:\Windows\System32 and the one in SysWOW64 to C:\Windows\SysWOW64 respectively. Grant access rights. (On 64-bit Windows you have to copy it with a 64-bit Programm, best by the Windows Explorer)
